title: Ranger 7 (Atlas-Agena B)
description: Open datasets and source materials relevant to Ranger 7 (1964), the first US close-up photographs of the lunar surface, Haemorhous cassinii (Cassin's Finch), and lunar imaging science

sources.name: NSSDC Master Catalog -- Ranger 7
sources.description: NASA Space Science Data Coordinated Archive entry for Ranger 7. Spacecraft ID: 1964-041A. Mission profile, spacecraft description (365.7 kg), six television camera specifications (two wide-angle, four narrow-angle), Atlas-Agena B launch vehicle details, impact coordinates in Mare Cognitum, and the 4,308 images transmitted during final 17.5 minutes of flight.
sources.mission_relevance: Definitive source for Ranger 7 spacecraft specifications, camera system layout, trajectory parameters, and mission timeline. Documents the first successful US close-up lunar photography and the impact point at 10.63°S, 20.68°W in Mare Cognitum.

sources.name: Planetary Data System -- Ranger 7 Image Archive
sources.description: Complete archive of all 4,308 Ranger 7 television images in calibrated digital format. Includes both full-scan (F-channel) and partial-scan (P-channel) images from all six cameras. Resolution ranges from approximately 2 km in the earliest images to 0.5 meters in the final frames before impact.
sources.mission_relevance: The images themselves — the primary scientific product of the mission. Essential for any analysis of lunar surface morphology at scales previously unobservable from Earth.

sources.name: JPL Technical Report 32-700 -- Ranger VII Photographs of the Moon
sources.description: The official JPL mission report documenting Ranger 7's camera system performance, image catalog, preliminary geological analysis of Mare Cognitum, and comparison with pre-mission telescopic observations. Published 1964-1965.
sources.mission_relevance: Primary technical documentation for the camera system design (six vidicon tubes, two wide-angle full-scan and four narrow-angle partial-scan), the image transmission protocol, and the geological interpretation of the 4,308 photographs.

sources.name: JPL HORIZONS Ephemeris System
sources.description: General-purpose ephemeris service. Can reconstruct the Moon's position on July 28-31, 1964, compute the Atlas-Agena B transfer trajectory, and determine the impact geometry at Mare Cognitum.
sources.mission_relevance: Enables trajectory reconstruction of the 68.6-hour Earth-Moon transit and the final approach geometry.

sources.name: USGS Astrogeology -- Lunar Geologic Maps
sources.description: USGS geological maps of the lunar surface, including the Mare Cognitum region where Ranger 7 impacted. Maps derived from Lunar Orbiter, Apollo, and Clementine data provide geological context for interpreting Ranger 7's images.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides the geological framework for Mare Cognitum and surrounding terrain. Ranger 7's photographs were the first data that informed these maps.

sources.name: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Camera (LROC)
sources.description: Modern high-resolution images of the Ranger 7 impact site from the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Camera. LROC Narrow Angle Camera resolution: 0.5 m/pixel — comparable to Ranger 7's best frames but covering the same terrain 50+ years later.
sources.mission_relevance: Allows direct comparison between Ranger 7's 1964 photographs and modern imagery. The LROC has imaged the Ranger 7 impact crater itself.

sources.name: eBird -- Cassin's Finch (Haemorhous cassinii)
sources.description: Citizen science observation database for Cassin's Finch across North America. Contains distribution maps, seasonal abundance charts, and elevational profiles. The eBird data confirms the species' preference for montane coniferous forests above 1,200 m elevation in the PNW.
sources.mission_relevance: Primary distribution and abundance data for the mission's organism pairing. The elevational profile — peaking at 1,800-2,200 m in the Cascades — connects to the altitude-resolution relationship in Ranger 7's imaging: both the bird and the camera achieve their best performance at specific altitudes.

sources.name: Birds of the World -- Cassin's Finch
sources.description: Comprehensive species account including morphology, song analysis, breeding biology, elevational distribution, and population dynamics. Contains spectrograms of the complex warbling song that distinguishes Cassin's Finch from House and Purple Finches.
sources.mission_relevance: Detailed species biology for the organism pairing. Song complexity and the spectrographic analysis of vocalizations connect to Ranger 7's image resolution — both are data that reveals structure invisible to casual observation.

sources.name: NASA Image Library -- Ranger 7
sources.description: NASA's curated collection of Ranger 7 mission photographs, including selected lunar surface images, spacecraft assembly photos, launch coverage, and JPL control room documentation.
sources.mission_relevance: Visual documentation of the mission from assembly through impact. Includes the iconic images of the JPL mission control team's reaction when the first photographs appeared on screen.

sources.name: USDA FEIS -- Pinus ponderosa (Ponderosa Pine)
sources.description: Fire Effects Information System account for ponderosa pine — the primary forest type in which Cassin's Finch breeds in the PNW. Includes habitat descriptions, fire ecology, and forest structure data relevant to the bird's elevational preferences.
sources.mission_relevance: Ecological context for Cassin's Finch habitat. The open canopy structure of ponderosa pine forests — widely spaced trees with exposed crowns — mirrors the open terrain of Mare Cognitum as revealed by Ranger 7.
